title: Deprecated numpy aliases of builtin types should not be used
description: Using numpy aliases for built-in types can lead to unexpected behavior, especially when the numpy version is updated.
message: Using numpy alias for built-in type: {{ type }}
recommendation: Use built-in types instead of numpy aliases

noncompliant.code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype='int8')
noncompliant.description: Using np.array with built-in type int8.

compliant.code:
import numpy as np
arr = np.array([1, 2, 3], dtype=np.int32)

why_is_this_an_issue: Using numpy aliases for built-in types can lead to unexpected behavior, especially when the numpy version is updated.
how_to_fix: Use built-in types instead of numpy aliases

checks.message: np.array call with forbidden built-in dtype alias.

logic.check_type_doc: Uses a custom function to robustly detect np.array calls with dtype set to a forbidden built-in type alias (int, float, str, bool, complex).
